Does the inlet/outlet pipe exhibit signs of damage or plugging? (Look for cracks in the piping and compacted soil and debris inside the pipe)

entry_values.text: No issues observed
Inlet/Outlet piping is partially clogged with sediment or debris
Inlet/Outlet piping is totally clogged with sediment or debris
Inlet/Outlet piping exhibits crack or damage will potentially affect its functionality

entries.label: Does the forebay exhibit signs of sediment accumulation ? (The forebay is a type of pre-treatment designed to capture trash and coarse sediment prior to stormwater reaching the filter bed) (Approximate percentage of accumulation within the structure relative to the height of  outflow piping)

entry_values.text: Facility does not have a forebay

entries.label: Does the forebay have an accumulation of sediment? (The forebay is a type of pre-treatment designed to capture trash and coarse sediment prior to stormwater reaching the filter bed)

entry_values.text: Facility does not have a forebay
Sediment accumulation is less that 6 inches
Sediment accumulation is greater that 6 inches

entries.label: Does the forebay exhibit signs of  undesirable vegetation? (Inspect for vegetation that would jeopardize the integrity of the forebay, such as woody vegetation or vegetation that will make sediment clean out difficult)

entry_values.text: Facility does not have a forebay
Forebay does not exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation
Forebay does exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation

entries.label: Does the forebay exhibit signs of erosion? (Also look for indications of animal burrows. This can also contribute to future erosion problems)

entry_values.text: Facility does not have a forebay
Forebay does not exhibit signs of erosion
Minor rill erosion observed
Significant erosion observed

entries.label: Does the low-flow channel (the channel in which stormwater flows from the inflow pipe to the control structure) (if present) exhibit signs of sediment accumulation?

entry_values.text: Basin does not have a low-flow channel
Sediment accumulation is less than 3 inches
Sediment accumulation is more than 3 inches

entries.label: Does the low-flow channel exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation?

entry_values.text: Facility does not have a low-flow channel
Facility does not exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation
Facility does exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation

entries.label: Does the low-flow channel exhibit signs of erosion? (Also look for indications of animal burrows. This can also contribute to future erosion problems)

entry_values.text: Facility does not have a low-flow channel
Minor erosion observed
Significant erosion observed

entries.label: Is the water level in the basin at the designed water level? (The water should be at or near the invert of the drawdown device except 24 hours after a storm event and during prolonged dry periods. If the water is level is higher, the outlet could be clogged.)

entry_values.text: The water level is at or near the invert of the drawdown device
The water level is higher than invert of the drawdown device but a rain event has occurred within 24 hours after a rain event
The water level is lower than the invert of the drawdrown device but it is during a prolonged dry period.
The water level is not at or near the invert of the drawndown device and the outlet is clogged.
The water level is not at or near the invert of the drawndown device and the outlet is clear of debris.

entries.label: Does the wetland zones exhibit signs of sediment accumulation? (Approximate percentage of accumulation within the structure relative to the height of outflow piping)
Does the wetland zones exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation?

entry_values.text: Facility does not exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation
Facility does exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation

entries.label: Does the basin exhibit signs of trash or debris accumulation?

entry_values.text: Trash or debris not observed in the basin
Minor trash and debris observed in basin
Significant trash and debris observed in basin

entries.label: Does the wetland zones exhibit signs of trash or debris accumulation?

entry_values.text: Trash or debris not observed in the basin
Minor trash and debris observed in basin
Significant trash and debris observed in basin

entries.label: Does the basin (main filter bed) have accumulated trash and debris?

entry_values.text: Trash or debris not observed in the basin
Minor trash and debris observed in basin
Significant trash and debris observed in basin

entries.label: Does the vegetation in the basin look healthy?

entry_values.text: Yes, vegetation looks healthy
<33% of the vegetation appears to be dead or dying
>33% of the vegetation appears to be dead or dying

entries.label: Does the basin exhibit signs of sediment accumulation?

entry_values.text: Sediment accumulation is less than 3 inches
Sediment accumulation is more than 3 inches

entries.label: Does the basin exhibit signs of erosion? (Also look for indications of animal burrows. This can also contribute to future erosion problems)

entry_values.text: Basin does not exhibit signs of erosion
Minor rill erosion observed
Significant erosion observed

entries.label: Does the basin exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation?

entry_values.text: Facility does not exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation
Facility does exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation

entries.label: Does the basin exhibit signs of undesirable vegetation (Inspect for vegetation that would jeopardize the integrity of the underdrain, such as woody vegetation or vegetation that will make sediment cleanout difficult)

entry_values.text: Basin does not exhibit growth of undesirable vegetation
Basin exhibits growth of undesirable vegetation that may damage the underdrain or prevent the basin from properly draining

entries.label: Does the basin have standing water? (The basin should typically drain within 72 hours after a storm event. If the basin has standing water, check the outlet structure for clogging.)

entry_values.text: Basin is free of standing water
Basin has standing water but was preceded by a storm event within 72 hours
Basin has standing water and there appears to be a clog in the outlet structure
Basin has standing water and the outlet structure is dry.

entries.label: Does the basin have standing water? (The basin should typically drain within 48 hours after a storm event.  If the basin has standing water check the outlet control structure for clogging)

entry_values.text: Basin is free of standing water
Basin has standing water but was preceded by a storm event within 24 hours
Basin has standing water but was preceded by a storm event within 24 hours
Basin has standing water and there appears to be a clog in the outlet structure
Basin has standing water and the outlet control structure is dry and free of debris.

entries.label: Does the basin have algal growth?

entry_values.text: The basin has less than 50% algal growth
The basin has more than 50% algal growth

entries.label: Does the basin have accumulated sediment?

entry_values.text: Sediment accumulation is less that 3 inches
Sediment accumulation is greater that 3 inches

entries.label: Does the fill embankment exhibit growth of woody vegetation (shrub and trees)? (Embankments can either be fill or cut. A fill embankment is an embankment that has been built-up. A cut embankment is an embankment that has been cut into existing ground.)

entry_values.text: The embankment does not display growth of woody vegetation
The embankment displays growth of woody vegetation

entries.label: Does the fill embankment exhibit signs of erosion? (Also look for indications of animal burrows. This can also contribute to future erosion problems)

entry_values.text: Basin does not exhibit signs of erosion
Minor erosion observed
Significant erosion observed

entries.label: Does the emergency spillway have trash, debris, or undesirable vegetation in the emergency spillway? (In an event that an uncommon storm event (etc. a 100 year flood) would occur, the emergency spillway is designed to protect the embankment by allowing stormwater to flow over the emergency spillway.)

entry_values.text: The basin does not have an emergency spillway
The emergency spillway does not have trash, debris or undesirable vegetation.
The emergency spillway does have trash, debris and/or undesirable vegetation.

entries.label: Does the vegetation on the fill embankment look healthy?

entry_values.text: Yes, vegetation looks healthy
<33% of the vegetation appears to be dead or dying
>33% of the vegetation appears to be dead or dying

entries.label: Is there any woody vegetation within 25' of the control structure (The area should be free of woody vegetation including the low-flow pipe if it extends in front of the control structure and is covered in rip-rap)

entry_values.text: The control structure and associated low-flow piping is free of woody vegetation
There is woody vegetation within 25 feet of the control structure which could potentially jeopardize the structural integrity

entries.label: Is the control structure's trash rack in good condition? (Is there trash or debris in it? Is the trash rack damaged or corroded? Is it allowing for positive drainage into the structure? Also assess inside the control structure)

entry_values.text: The control structure does not have a trash rack
The trash rack is in good condition
The trash rack is in fair condition
The trash rack is in poor condition

entries.label: Is the outfall area clear of debris and sediment and allowing for positive drainage? (Look inside the outfall piping, assess the condition, if it is metal is it rusted or deteriorating?)

entry_values.text: The outfall is clear of debris and sediment and allows for positive drainage
The outfall allows for drainage but the piping material appears to be compromised and presents a potential risk for failure
The outfall allows for drainage but moderate sediment accumulation is observed and presents a potential problem if worsens
Piping does not allow for positive drainage, potentially clogged or collapsed

entries.label: Does the safety bench exhibit signs of erosion? (A safety bench is bench located on the perimeter of the permanent pool to allow for maintenance access)

entry_values.text: The facility does not have a safety bench
The safety bench does not display signs of erosion
Minor erosion observed
Significant erosion observed.

entries.label: Does the safety and aquatic benches exhibit growth of dead, unhealthy, or undesirable plant material? (An aquatic bench is a shallow area inside the perimeter of the normal pool the promotes growth of aquatic plants)

entry_values.text: The facility does not have a safety bench or aquatic bench
Vegetation looks healthy and is free of undesirable plants
<33% of the vegetation appears to be dead or dying
>33% of the vegetation appears to be dead or dying and/or undesirable plants present

entries.label: Is mulch cover in place and uniform throughout the basin?

entry_values.text: Yes, mulch cover is in place and uniform
Mulch cover is inconsistent or patchy
Mulch cover is non-existent or washed away

entries.label: Are underdrain cleanout caps missing or damaged?

entry_values.text: Cleanout caps are present and in good condition
Cleanout caps are missing or damaged

entries.label: Does the wetland zones have algal growth?

entry_values.text: The basin has less than 50% algal growth
The basin has more than 50% algal growth

entries.label: Does the vegetation in the wetland zones look healthy?

entry_values.text: Yes, vegetation looks healthy
<33% of the vegetation appears to be dead or dying
>33% of the vegetation appears to be dead or dying

entries.label: Is the water level in the wetland zones at the designed water level? (The water should be at or near the outlet/orifice opening. If water is above the outlet/orifice opening, the drawdown device could be clogged.)

entry_values.text: The water level is at or near the outlet/orifice opening
The water level is higher than the outlet/orfice opening and the outlet structure appears to be clogged
The water level is higher than the outlet/orifice opening and the outlet structure does not appear to be clogged

entries.label: Does the flashboard riser (if present) exhibit signs of damage or plugging? (Look for cracks in the piping and compacted soil and debris inside the pipe). (A flashboard is a board that is installed over a riser to maintain water at a desired height before spilling over into the culvert)

entry_values.text: Facility does not have a flashboard riser
No issues observed
Flashboard is partially clogged with sediment or debris
Flashboard is totally clogged with sediment or debris
Flashboard exhibits crack or damage will potentially affect its functionality

entries.label: BMP Summary

entry_values.text: 1 - System operating as designed - No issues
2 - System operating as designed - Maintenance needed
3 - System operating as designed - Minor repairs needed
4 - System not operating as designed - Major repairs needed
5 - System not operating as designed - Safety issues and major repairs needed
